ogTitle: Cremorne Point vs Crestwood: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cremorne Point and Crestwood.
heading: Cremorne Point vs Crestwood — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cremorne Point, New South Wales and Crestwood, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Crestwood has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Cremorne Point (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cremorne Point or Crestwood safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cremorne Point has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Crestwood scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cremorne Point scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Crestwood scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cremorne Point or Crestwood?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Crestwood has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
